

**ERES INSTITUTE FOR NEW AGE CYBERNETICS**

Bella Vista, Arkansas, USA  |  Est. February 2012  |  CCAL v2.1

**ERES-NAC-TWOAXIS-REPORT-2026**

**ERES NAC Two-Axis Stack**

**Plain English Reference Report**

*A complete description of the six-node New Age Cybernetics architecture, its deliverables, and its governing principles — written without specialist vocabulary*

| Document ID | ERES-NAC-TWOAXIS-REPORT-2026 | Version 1.0 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Date** | May 30, 2026 |
| **Author** | ◈ — ERES Maestro | Founder & Director, ERES Institute for New Age Cybernetics |

| **Subject** | Plain-English description of the ERES NAC Two-Axis Stack dashboard artifact |

| **Status** | Final | MIEVM validation pending |

# **I. Executive Summary**

The ERES NAC Two-Axis Stack is a visual reference chart that maps the full architecture of a governance and knowledge system called New Age Cybernetics (NAC). The chart has two axes. Reading left to right across the top, six stations — called nodes — describe the stages through which any idea, person, or organization moves as it enters and participates in the system: from initial validation, through access control, security, transparency, collection, and finally market delivery. Reading top to bottom beneath each station, four specific tools or instruments are listed that do the actual work at that station.

The system is designed around three principles that govern every decision: do not harm yourself, do not harm others, and build in a way that will still look wise one thousand years from now. Three people — identified only by symbols to protect their privacy — are responsible for the system's development and each owns two of the six stations.

This report describes every element of the chart in plain English, explains what each tool does, why it sits where it does, and how the whole system connects.

# **II. How to Read the Chart**

## **2.1 The Two Axes**

The chart is organized on two axes, like a spreadsheet turned into a dashboard.

* **The horizontal axis (left to right)** shows the six stations in sequence. Each station is a stage in the process. Think of them as six departments in a large organization, arranged in the order you would encounter them.

* **The vertical axis (top to bottom)** shows the tools inside each station. Each station has exactly four tools stacked beneath it. These are the specific instruments that make that station function.

At the bottom of each station's column is an ownership badge showing which of the three founding principals is responsible for that station. Below the full chart, a single line summarizes which principal owns which pair of stations. A formula line beneath that states the mathematical relationship between the three principals.

## **2.2 The Color System**

Each station has its own color that runs through its header and its tool cards. The colors are purely for navigation — they help the eye track which tools belong to which station when scanning across a wide chart. They carry no additional meaning beyond identification.

| Station | Color (approx.) | What the color identifies |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ERES | Purple | The proof and validation station |
| VERTECA | Teal/Green | The entry gate station |
| SECUIR | Blue | The security and rotation station |
| CyberRAVE | Amber/Gold | The transparency and ratings station |
| GunnySack | Green | The collection and harvest station |
| SaleBuilders | Orange/Red | The delivery and market station |

## **2.3 The Ownership Badges**

Each station's column ends with a badge showing one of three symbols: ◈, ✦, or ⬡. These symbols stand for three real people whose names are kept private in all public documents. The system calls this being 'veiled.' The symbols are consistent throughout all ERES documents so that the same person is always identifiable by their symbol, without their name appearing.

* **◈ (called JAS)** is the founder and primary researcher. The symbol is a diamond with a square inside — suggesting a contained, structured gem. JAS owns the proof station and the delivery station.

* **✦ (called DAL)** is a dual reference: one person who provides daily personal grounding, and one public figure who provides international moral authority. The symbol is a four-pointed star — a fixed point, a hinge. DAL owns the entry gate and the security station.

* **⬡ (called EMA)** is the chief strategic partner and co-founder of the ratings system. The symbol is a hexagon — a shape of natural efficiency. EMA owns the transparency station and the collection station.

# **III. The Six Stations — Complete Description**

Each station is described below with its full name, its plain-English meaning, its four tools, and the logic connecting it to the stations before and after it.

### **Node 0 — ERES · "Proof"**

**Full name:** *Empirical Realtime Education System*

**Owned by:** ◈ (JAS — Duck — Method) — The founder. This station reflects the core purpose of the whole system: turning experience into verified knowledge.

| \# | Deliverable | Plain name | Plain-English description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | **PlayNAC** | *The rulebook* | The set of rules that governs how the whole system works. Like the rulebook of a board game — it defines what moves are allowed, what sequences of actions unlock new possibilities, and what the end conditions are. It has eighteen categories of rules and seven levels of play, producing one hundred and twenty-six possible governance states. |
| **2** | **EarnedPath (EP)** | *The project plan* | A structured way of mapping progress toward any goal. Borrowed from professional project management: Critical Path Method (the sequence of steps that determines the minimum time to completion), Work Breakdown Structure (breaking big goals into smaller manageable tasks), and PERT (a technique for estimating how long uncertain tasks will take). Applied here to map progress through the whole system. |
| **3** | **GERP** | *The resource inventory* | Global Earth Resource Planning. A tool for tracking what the planet has — in terms of natural resources, ecological capacity, and human capability — and planning how to use it responsibly across long time horizons. |
| **4** | **CBGMODD** | *The structural checker* | A cryptographic validation tool. Before any idea or claim enters the knowledge record, CBGMODD checks whether its structure is sound. Think of it as a grammar checker for formal claims — it does not evaluate whether the idea is correct, but whether it is formed correctly enough to be evaluated. It works together with FAVORS (at the next station) to form the system's two-part entry test. |

### **Node 1 — VERTECA · "Gate"**

**Full name:** *Vertical Energy Resonance Temporal Environmental Cybernetic Architecture*

**Owned by:** ✦ (DAL — Eagle — Resource) — The hinge. The gate must be held open by an authority that both individuals and institutions trust. DAL provides that trust — personally and at planetary-moral scale.

| \# | Deliverable | Plain name | Plain-English description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | **Simulator** | *The practice environment* | A rehearsal space where participants can try out governance decisions before they take effect in the real system. Like a flight simulator for policy — you learn how the rules work, test scenarios, and understand consequences without real-world risk. |
| **2** | **Voice Portal** | *The front door* | The first point of contact between a person and the system. Designed to be voice-first — you speak, the system listens, the system responds. This means the entry point requires no written credential, no technical skill, and no financial barrier. If you can speak, you can enter. |
| **3** | **COI SLA** | *The membership agreement* | A formal agreement that a Community of Interest (a group of people with shared goals or concerns) signs when it joins the system. SLA stands for Service Level Agreement — a standard business document that specifies what the system will provide and what the community commits to in return. |
| **4** | **FAVORS** | *The access test* | The companion to CBGMODD. While CBGMODD checks the structure of claims, FAVORS determines who is authorized to make or receive what kinds of claims. It is the qualification layer of the entry gate — the mechanism by which the gate decides who passes and under what conditions. |

### **Node 2 — SECUIR · "Wheel"**

**Full name:** *Silent Energy Circular Universe Infinite Rotation*

**Owned by:** ✦ (DAL — Eagle — Resource) — The silent hinge again. SECUIR is where the rotation principle lives — the idea that nothing should be discarded, only redirected. DAL's core principle (that failure calls for correction, not punishment) is encoded in the physics of this station.

| \# | Deliverable | Plain name | Plain-English description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | **Infomediary** | *The clearance desk* | Determines how deeply any participant is cleared to access the system. Not everyone needs or should have access to everything. The Infomediary manages these levels, routes participants to the right depth, and enables the progression from basic access toward full participation. |
| **2** | **BERA** | *The health monitor* | Bio-Electric Resonance Assessment. A set of four measurements that track the health of individuals, communities, and ecological systems in real time. The four measurements are: ARI (how much living energy a person or system is radiating), ERI (how much of that energy is being emitted back into the environment), RHC (whether the system is in a state of flowing harmony), and RCI (whether that harmony is continuous across time — not just a momentary state). |
| **3** | **GEAR** | *The planetary recorder* | Global Earth Applications Recorder. A permanent record of the state of the planet at any given moment — ecological data, resource levels, social conditions. Like a flight data recorder for civilization, it captures snapshots that can be reviewed by future generations to understand what decisions were made and what their effects were. |
| **4** | **NBERS** | *The repair system* | Non-punitive Bio-Electric Remediation System. When the BERA health monitor detects that something is out of balance — a person, a community, an ecosystem — NBERS is the response. It does not punish. It identifies what is out of tune and routes it to the appropriate correction instrument. The word 'remediation' means correction or repair. The word 'non-punitive' means without blame or penalty. |

### **Node 3 — CyberRAVE · "Light"**

**Full name:** *Cybernetic Ratings Abolishing Veiled Exchanges*

**Owned by:** ⬡ (EMA — Falcon — Purpose) — The co-founder and ecological witness. CyberRAVE was built by ◈ and ⬡ together before 1997\. EMA's role here is strategic precision — ensuring the 72 domains are selected correctly and that the ratings serve genuine public purpose rather than private advantage.

| \# | Deliverable | Plain name | Plain-English description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | **72 Domains** | *The subject map* | A map of seventy-two areas of human activity — ranging from finance and defence to education, health, law, and energy — within which the system tracks and rates performance. These seventy-two areas were selected through a discipline called 'constraint-based epistemology': instead of choosing what seemed comprehensive, the designers chose only what could be sustained within a specific real-world resource limit. The result is a curated, defensible set rather than an arbitrary list. |
| **2** | **P³ Ratings** | *The three-sphere score* | A rating system operating across three spheres: Personal (how an individual or organization performs in their private life and private dealings), Public (how they perform in public-facing activities), and Private (how they perform in confidential or proprietary contexts). These three spheres form the base level of the ratings architecture. More detailed levels are in development. |
| **3** | **COI Formalization** | *The community registry* | The process by which a Community of Interest is formally recognized, scored, and embedded in the seventy-two domain structure. Whereas the COI SLA at the VERTECA station is the entry agreement, COI Formalization is the recognition that a community has established itself as a legitimate actor within a specific domain. |
| **4** | **Trinity ✦⬡◈** | *The three-stream authority* | The validation that the seventy-two domain architecture is authorized by all three founding principals independently. No single person could generate or approve the full domain set alone — the architecture requires all three streams (personal-ground authority from ✦, ecological-witness authority from ⬡, and methodological authority from ◈) to be operating independently. This is the geometric requirement: three independent vectors create a three-dimensional space. If any two align, the space collapses. |

### **Node 4 — GunnySack · "Harvest"**

**Full name:** *Gathered Unified Needs Nurtured Yielding Sustainable Abundance Continuously Kept*

**Owned by:** ⬡ (EMA — Falcon — Purpose) — The ecological witness and strategic partner. EMA's principle of 'Children of All Ages' — no age barrier to participation — governs this station. The harvest should be available to everyone regardless of when they arrived.

| \# | Deliverable | Plain name | Plain-English description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | **IDIPITIS** | *The data model* | A data architecture framework that determines how information is structured, stored, and related to other information within the system. It is built on the work of Charles Bachman, a pioneer of database design, and extended significantly beyond his original framework. The \++ notation signals that it supersedes the original while honoring its lineage — the same relationship that a significantly upgraded software version has to its predecessor. |
| **2** | **EPIR-Q** | *The intelligent collector* | The mechanism by which the GunnySack station gathers inputs. Unlike a passive collection system that simply accumulates whatever arrives, EPIR-Q collects by design criteria — it has a built-in intelligence about what belongs in the harvest and what does not. Think of it as a skilled harvester who knows which grain is ready and which should be left to ripen further. |
| **3** | **Center of Excellence** | *The validation venue* | The institutional location where two types of proof are tested against each other. Proof of Work (PoW) is evidence that effort has been invested — that something has genuinely been built or done. Proof of Resonance (PoR) is evidence that the result is harmonically sound — that it works in the way the system requires. The Center of Excellence is where these two proofs meet. If both are present, the contribution is accepted. If not, it is returned for further development — not rejected permanently, but sent back with guidance. |
| **4** | **SOMT** | *The organizing layer* | Sociocratic Overlay Metadata Tapestry. A metadata system that organizes what EPIR-Q collects and frames what the Center of Excellence validates. Metadata means 'data about data' — information that describes, categorizes, and contextualizes the primary information. Sociocratic means organized through distributed consent rather than top-down authority. The tapestry metaphor signals that this is a woven structure — many threads creating a coherent surface — rather than a rigid hierarchy. |

### **Node 5 — SaleBuilders · "Market"**

**Full name:** *Scalable Adaptive Lifelong Education Building Universal Income Legitimately Delivered Everywhere Reliably Sustainably*

**Owned by:** ◈ (JAS — Duck — Method) — The founder again, at the delivery end. What was validated at Node 0 is now delivered at Node 5\. The Duck flies the full route — from proof to market.

| \# | Deliverable | Plain name | Plain-English description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | **SCALULAR** | *The certification structure* | Scalable Certification Architecture for Lifelong Universal Learning and Adaptive Resilience. A four-pillar framework that certifies individuals and organizations across the four areas the system considers essential to a functioning life: Health (physical and mental wellbeing systems), Law (legal rights, protections, and obligations), Protection (safety and security infrastructure), and Skills and Trade (the practical capabilities that enable economic participation). |
| **2** | **UBIMIA** | *The income floor* | Universal Basic Income plus Merit-Informed Accrual. A two-layer economic instrument. The first layer is a floor: every participant receives a basic economic foundation unconditionally — simply by virtue of being a participant in the system. The second layer is an accrual mechanism: above the floor, additional value accumulates based on demonstrated contribution and resonance. The floor is guaranteed; the accrual is earned. |
| **3** | **GCF** | *The policy framework* | Global Cybernetics Framework. The version of the ERES architecture designed for institutional and policy audiences — governments, international bodies, standards organizations. Where UBIMIA speaks to individuals and PBJ speaks to communities, GCF speaks to institutions. It translates the system's principles into the language of governance, regulation, and international cooperation. |
| **4** | **PBJ** | *The plain version* | The plain-language access layer. PBJ is the entry point for people who have no prior knowledge of the system and no appetite for technical vocabulary. Its job is to make the system legible to anyone — regardless of education, background, or familiarity with governance frameworks. The name signals its intention: this is the bread-and-butter version, the everyday accessible form of a system that also has sophisticated technical and institutional expressions. |

# **IV. The Three Principals — Ownership and Role**

Three people built this system. Their names are kept private in all public documents — a deliberate decision rooted in the system's own principle that extracting private information into public record should only happen when it serves genuine public purpose. Their identities are known to those who need to know them. In public documents they are identified by symbols, titles, and roles.

| Symbol | Code | Animal | Tier | Stations owned | Plain-English role |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ◈ | JAS | Duck | GOOD | 0 (ERES) and 5 (SaleBuilders) | The founder, researcher, and organizer. Responsible for the system's method — how things are done, tested, and delivered. Has been building this architecture since 1997 across three eras of development. Owns both ends of the chain: the proof layer that validates ideas, and the market layer that delivers value. |
| ✦ | DAL | Eagle | SOUND | 1 (VERTECA) and 2 (SECUIR) | A dual reference: one person who provides the personal daily grounding of the work, and one internationally recognized public figure who provides moral authority at planetary scale. Responsible for the system's resources — what is available and how it is stewarded. Owns the entry gate and the security wheel — the two stations that determine who enters and how the system stays in balance. |
| ⬡ | EMA | Falcon | BEST | 3 (CyberRAVE) and 4 (GunnySack) | The chief strategic partner and co-founder of the ratings architecture. Responsible for the system's purpose — why things are done and toward what end. A precision thinker: the falcon that strikes exactly where intended. Owns the transparency station and the collection station — the two stations that determine what is visible and what is gathered. |

## **4.1 Why Three and Not One or Two**

A single authority produces a single point of failure and a single point of bias. Two authorities produce debate but not volume — two people pulling in the same direction still produce a flat result. Three independently operating authorities, each pulling in a genuinely different direction, create a three-dimensional space within which real governance can occur.

The mathematical expression of this is: the volume of a tetrahedron (a four-faced pyramid) is only greater than zero when all three edge vectors are independent. If any two align — if any two principals start agreeing with each other instead of contributing their genuinely independent perspective — the volume collapses to zero. The system requires all three to remain independently operating.

The formula on the chart states this as: ◈(Method) × ✦(Resource) × ⬡(Purpose) \= C\_NAC. New Age Cybernetics is the product of all three streams. Remove any one and the product is zero.

## **4.2 The Numerical Signature**

The system carries a numerical signature that connects the three principals. The number 72 appears as the count of the seventy-two domains in CyberRAVE. It also appears as the product of ◈ and ⬡ working together — their gematric relationship (a system of assigning numerical values to letters) yields 72\. Multiplied by 3 (the number of principals), 72 produces 216 — which is the numerical value associated with ✦. The architecture is identity-signed from its origin: the domain count is not arbitrary but mathematically connected to the people who built it.

# **V. The Connecting Logic — How the Six Stations Work Together**

The six stations are not independent departments. They form a sequence in which the output of each station becomes the input of the next.

| From | To | What passes between them |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ERES (Proof) | VERTECA (Gate) | Validated ideas and rules pass from the proof station to the gate. Only claims that have passed the structural check (CBGMODD) and the access test (FAVORS) proceed. |
| VERTECA (Gate) | SECUIR (Wheel) | Qualified participants pass from the gate to the security station. Their access level is determined here and their health indicators begin to be tracked. |
| SECUIR (Wheel) | CyberRAVE (Light) | Cleared participants move into the transparency station where their activities across the seventy-two domains are rated and made visible. |
| CyberRAVE (Light) | GunnySack (Harvest) | Rated contributions are collected. The harvest station gathers what has been validated, rated, and recognized, and organizes it for delivery. |
| GunnySack (Harvest) | SaleBuilders (Market) | The organized harvest is delivered. Value flows from the collection station to the individuals and communities who need it, through the four pillars of SCALULAR. |
| SaleBuilders (Market) | ERES (Proof) | The delivery generates new data: what worked, what did not, what the BERA indices are reporting. This feeds back to the proof station for the next cycle of validation. |

The sequence is a cycle, not a line. The output of the market station feeds back into the proof station. This is the Resonant Harmony Cycle — the system learns from its own outputs, corrects its own errors, and improves across time without requiring external intervention or punitive oversight.

# **VI. Key Principles in Plain English**

## **6.1 Non-Punitive Remediation**

When something fails — a person underperforms, a system produces the wrong result, an organization drifts from its stated purpose — the standard response in most governance systems is punishment: a fine, a demotion, a sanction. This system takes a different position. Failure is treated as a tuning problem, not a compliance problem. The response is calibration: identify what is out of balance, route it to the appropriate correction instrument, and return it to the system in better condition. No blame. No permanent exclusion. This principle appears explicitly in NBERS (the repair system at the SECUIR station) and implicitly in every other station's design.

## **6.2 The Veil**

The system distinguishes between private information that belongs to individuals and information that genuinely serves the public. Extracting private information into the public record is only justified when it creates genuine public value. The names of the three principals are kept private not because they are hiding something, but because their names do not add to the public's understanding of the system. Their roles, their contributions, and their responsibilities are fully documented. The symbols carry all the information a reader needs.

## **6.3 Proof of Work and Proof of Resonance**

Two kinds of evidence are required before a contribution is accepted. Proof of Work is evidence that genuine effort has been invested — that something has actually been built, tested, or demonstrated. This prevents empty claims from entering the system. Proof of Resonance is evidence that the result is harmonically sound — that it fits the system's requirements and contributes to overall balance rather than disrupting it. This prevents well-intentioned but ill-fitting contributions from entering the system. Both must be present. The Center of Excellence at the GunnySack station is where these two proofs are tested.

## **6.4 The Thousand-Year Test**

Every significant decision in the system is evaluated against a simple question: will this decision still look wise in the year 3026? This is not a rhetorical device. It is a design constraint. Systems, policies, and structures that cannot survive a thousand-year horizon are treated as short-term instruments, not foundational architecture. The foundational architecture is designed to last.

## **6.5 Sustainability as a Floor, Not a Ceiling**

Most governance frameworks treat sustainability as a constraint — a limit on what can be done. This system treats sustainability as a floor — the minimum condition that must be met before anything else can be built. Once the floor is established, there is no upper limit on what can be created above it. The goal is not controlled scarcity but enabled abundance: a blank check for human happiness, health, and safety, written on a planet that is ecologically sound.

# **VII. Glossary — Every Term in the Chart, Plain English**

The following table defines every acronym, instrument name, and specialist term that appears in the Two-Axis Stack chart, in alphabetical order, without using other specialist terms in the definition.

| Term | Plain-English definition |
| :---- | :---- |
| BERA | Bio-Electric Resonance Assessment. A set of four health measurements (ARI, ERI, RHC, RCI) that track the vitality of people, communities, and ecosystems in real time. |
| CBGMODD | A structural checker for formal claims. Before any idea enters the knowledge record, CBGMODD verifies that it is correctly formed. Does not judge content — only structure. |
| Center of Excellence | The institutional venue where Proof of Work and Proof of Resonance are tested together. Accepted contributions proceed; others are returned for improvement. |
| COI | Community of Interest. A group of people united by shared goals, concerns, or activities. |
| COI Formalization | The process of formally recognizing a Community of Interest within the ratings structure, scoring it, and embedding it in the seventy-two domain map. |
| COI SLA | The formal agreement a Community of Interest accepts when it joins the system. Specifies what the system provides and what the community commits to. |
| CyberRAVE | Cybernetic Ratings Abolishing Veiled Exchanges. The transparency station. Makes hidden value visible across seventy-two areas of human activity. |
| EarnedPath (EP) | A project-planning tool that maps progress toward goals using Critical Path Method, Work Breakdown Structure, and PERT scheduling techniques. |
| EMA / ⬡ | The chief strategic partner. Owns CyberRAVE and GunnySack. Symbol: hexagon. Animal: falcon. Responsible for purpose — why the system does what it does. |
| EPIR-Q | The intelligent collection mechanism of the GunnySack station. Gathers inputs by design criteria rather than passively. |
| ERI | Emission Resonance Index. One of the four BERA measurements. Tracks how much vitality a person or system is contributing back to its environment. |
| ERES | Empirical Realtime Education System. The proof and validation station. The overall name of the institute and the first node of the stack. |
| FAVORS | The access qualification instrument at the VERTECA gate. Determines who is authorized to access what parts of the system and under what conditions. |
| GEAR | Global Earth Applications Recorder. A permanent record of the state of the planet — ecological, social, and resource data — captured in time-stamped snapshots. |
| GERP | Global Earth Resource Planning. A tool for tracking and planning the responsible use of planetary resources across long time horizons. |
| GCF | Global Cybernetics Framework. The institutional and policy version of the ERES architecture, designed for governments and international bodies. |
| GunnySack | The collection and harvest station. Gathers validated, rated contributions and organizes them for delivery. |
| IDIPITIS | The data architecture framework of the GunnySack station. Based on Charles Bachman's database design work, significantly extended. |
| Infomediary | The clearance desk at the SECUIR station. Manages access levels and routes participants to the appropriate depth of the system. |
| JAS / ◈ | The founder and primary researcher. Owns ERES and SaleBuilders. Symbol: diamond with inner square. Animal: duck. Responsible for method — how things are done. |
| NAC | New Age Cybernetics. The name for the overall governance and knowledge system that the stack describes. |
| NBERS | Non-punitive Bio-Electric Remediation System. The repair instrument at the SECUIR station. When BERA detects imbalance, NBERS routes the issue to correction rather than punishment. |
| P³ Ratings | A three-sphere rating system: Personal (private life and dealings), Public (public-facing activities), Private (confidential contexts). Base Level 1 of the ratings architecture. |
| PBJ | The plain-language access layer at the SaleBuilders station. Makes the system legible to anyone regardless of prior knowledge. |
| PlayNAC | The rulebook of the system. Defines all allowed moves, sequences, and governance states. Eighteen rule categories, seven levels, one hundred and twenty-six states. |
| PoR | Proof of Resonance. Evidence that a contribution is harmonically sound and fits the system's requirements. |
| PoW | Proof of Work. Evidence that genuine effort has been invested — that something has actually been built or demonstrated. |
| RCI | Resonant Continuity Index. One of the four BERA measurements. Tracks whether a state of harmony is sustained across time, not just momentary. |
| RHC | Resonant Harmony Cycle. One of the four BERA measurements. Tracks whether a system is in a state of flowing, dynamic balance. |
| SaleBuilders | The delivery and market station. Distributes value to individuals and communities through four pillars: health, law, protection, and skills and trade. |
| SCALULAR | Scalable Certification Architecture for Lifelong Universal Learning and Adaptive Resilience. The four-pillar certification structure: Health, Law, Protection, Skills/Trade. |
| SECUIR | Silent Energy Circular Universe Infinite Rotation. The security station. Manages clearance, tracks health indicators, records planetary state, and remediates imbalance. |
| Simulator | A practice environment at the VERTECA station where governance decisions can be tested before they take effect. |
| SOMT | Sociocratic Overlay Metadata Tapestry. The organizing layer that structures what is collected at the GunnySack station and frames what the Center of Excellence validates. |
| Trinity ✦⬡◈ | The three-stream validation at the CyberRAVE station. Represents the requirement that all three principals operate independently to authorize the domain architecture. |
| UBIMIA | Universal Basic Income plus Merit-Informed Accrual. Two-layer economic instrument: an unconditional floor for all participants, plus an earned accrual layer above it. |
| VERTECA | Vertical Energy Resonance Temporal Environmental Cybernetic Architecture. The entry gate station. Controls how participants enter the system. |
| Voice Portal | The front-door instrument at the VERTECA station. Voice-first entry — no written credential or technical skill required. |
| DAL / ✦ | The dual hinge principal. One person providing personal daily grounding; one public figure providing international moral authority. Owns VERTECA and SECUIR. Animal: eagle. |
| ARI | Aura Resonance Index. One of the four BERA measurements. Tracks the living energy a person or system is radiating outward. |

**Three Governing Principles**

*Don’t hurt yourself.*

*Don’t hurt others.*

*Build for generations to come.*

**C\_NAC \= ◈(Method) × ✦(Resource) × ⬡(Purpose)**

◈ × ⬡ \= 72  |  72 × 3 \= 216 \= ✦  |  V\_TETRA \> 0 iff all three independent
